name: Montana Capital Car Title Loans
description_short: Montana Capital Car Title Loans Los Angeles, CA — Montana Capital provides vehicle title-secured loans up to $50,000 with same-day funding, targeting bo...
description_long:
Montana Capital Car Title Loans® began operations in 2007 with a single North Hollywood, California location and has since expanded to nationwide operations. The company is licensed by the California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation (DFPI License No. 603F508) and holds NMLS ID 2249848. As of their latest reporting, they claim to have served more than 375,000 customers over their 19-year operating history.

The company specializes exclusively in vehicle title-secured loans, where borrowers pledge their car's title as collateral in exchange for cash advances. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000 (or up to 70% of vehicle value), with terms up to 36 months depending on state regulations. They advertise a streamlined 3-step process: submit an application, receive funds while keeping vehicle keys, and repay the loan to recover the title. Montana Capital markets itself as an alternative for borrowers with bad credit who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans, emphasizing approval based on vehicle equity and income rather than credit history.

Montana Capital distinguishes itself through transparency messaging and operational scale. The company highlights no prepayment penalties, origination fees up to 10%, and APRs up to 175% (capped by state law). Leadership includes a Chief Operating Officer with 10+ years of finance industry experience focused on compliance and customer protection. They offer 24/7 availability, online applications, and claim dedicated teams for compliance, underwriting, and customer support across active lending states.

However, the cost structure is significant: APRs reaching 175% and origination fees up to 10% create substantial borrowing costs. While the company emphasizes responsible lending and transparency, the product remains fundamentally high-cost emergency debt. The 404 error on the specific Los Angeles location page suggests potential operational or website maintenance issues. This is appropriate for true emergencies requiring immediate cash, but the high cost makes it unsuitable for routine borrowing.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Loan amounts up to $50,000, significantly higher than typical payday loans
No prepayment penalties, allowing borrowers to save on interest by paying early
Approval based on vehicle equity and income rather than credit score, accessible to bad-credit borrowers
Loan terms up to 36 months, providing longer repayment windows than payday alternatives
24/7 availability and online application process for convenience
Licensed and regulated by California DFPI with transparency-focused leadership
Keeps vehicle keys with borrower despite using title as collateral
cons: APRs up to 175% represent extremely high borrowing costs compared to traditional lending
Origination fees up to 10% of loan amount add significant upfront costs
Vehicle title held as collateral creates risk of vehicle loss if unable to repay
Subject to state-specific lending caps that may vary availability and terms
Website displayed 404 error for specific location, suggesting potential operational issues
best_for: Borrowers with bad credit needing emergency funds larger than typical payday loans ($2,000+)
Vehicle owners facing immediate cash shortages with no other financing options available
Consumers who can repay within 12-36 months and want to avoid payday loan debt cycles
Individuals specifically seeking title-based loans where vehicle equity determines eligibility
diagnosis: Montana Capital is best for borrowers with damaged credit who own vehicles and face genuine emergencies requiring $2,000+ in cash quickly. The critical caveat: APRs up to 175% and origination fees make this an extremely expensive borrowing option that should only be considered when no alternatives exist and repayment within 12-36 months is certain.
services: Multiple loan term options (up to 36 months)
Title loan FAQs and educational resources
Loans for borrowers with bad credit history
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1601 S La Brea Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90019
